questions.question: In obsessive-compulsive disorder, compulsions are best described as:

options.text: Enjoyable rituals that the person chooses to perform for pleasure
Repetitive behaviors or mental acts performed to reduce the anxiety caused by obsessions
Involuntary muscle movements caused by neurological damage
Delusional beliefs about contamination or danger

questions.explanation: Compulsions are repetitive behaviors (such as hand washing or checking) or mental acts (such as counting or praying) that a person feels driven to perform in response to an obsession. They aim to reduce anxiety, but the relief is temporary and reinforces the cycle.

questions.question: A person who is preoccupied with perceived flaws in their physical appearance that are not observable to others most likely has:
questions.explanation: Body dysmorphic disorder involves a preoccupation with one or more perceived defects in physical appearance that are not observable or appear slight to others. It is classified as an obsessive-compulsive and related disorder in the DSM-5.
